title: Node.js URL Shortener API
description: A backend REST API built with Node.js, Express, and TypeScript. The application contains bugs in its URL format validator, redirects, analytics collection race conditions, and misses a basic rate-limiting middleware.

tasks.title: Fix URL Regex Validation Pattern
tasks.description: The URL input validation regex rejects valid URLs or accepts malformed URLs (e.g., missing scheme). Fix the validation logic to reject inputs that are not valid absolute HTTP/HTTPS URLs.

tasks.title: Fix Redirect Location Headers
tasks.description: The redirect endpoint GET /:code currently returns a status code of 200 or fails to set the correct Location headers, causing clients not to redirect. Fix this to redirect with a proper 302 Found status.

tasks.title: Fix Concurrent Analytics Counters
tasks.description: When multiple requests access the redirect code, the analytics counter updates incorrectly due to mutating state variables sequentially. Resolve the analytical update logic to safely track hit counts.

tasks.title: Implement Client IP Rate Limiting
tasks.description: Implement a custom rate-limiting middleware for POST /shorten that limits requests to 5 requests per minute per IP address. Return a 429 Too Many Requests response if exceeded.
